title: brassica oleracea subvar. viridis
intro: Brassica oleracea subvar. viridis, commonly known as kale, is a cool-season crop that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The health benefits of kale include lowering cholesterol levels, boosting the immune system, and providing essential vitamins and minerals such as vitamins A, C, and K, as well as calcium and iron. Kale can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being consumed as a raw or cooked vegetable in salads, soups, and other dishes.
